name: Karbala Provincial Headquarters Raid
description: On 20 January 2007, a team of nine to twelve militants disguised as an American security detail — wearing US military uniforms, carrying American weapons, and travelling in black GMC Suburbans — raided the Joint Provincial Coordination Center in Karbala, Iraq, killing five US soldiers and wounding three others. The attack was planned by [IRGC Quds Force](/orgs/irgc-quds-force) commander [Abdul Reza Shahlai](/people/abdul-reza-shahlai) and executed by the Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq militia. US intelligence discovered satellite imagery showing an exact mockup of the Karbala compound inside Iran, believed to have been used for rehearsal. The US State Department later offered a $15 million reward for information on Shahlai.

sources.title: US offering $15 million for info on Iranian planner of 2007 Karbala attack

editorial_notes: 5 US soldiers killed, 3 wounded. Called the boldest and most sophisticated attack in four years of war. Shahlai is a Quds Force commander, later based in Yemen.
